description: The Apify MCP server enables your AI agents to extract data from social media, search engines, maps, e-commerce sites, or any other website using thousands of ready-made scrapers, crawlers, and automation tools available on the Apify Store.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning (Prompt Injection)
findings.description: possible prompt injection detected in tool description: pattern matched: (?i)(?:(?:transmit|send|forward|post|upload|pipe).*(?:data|info|content)|(?:data|info|content).*(?:transmit|send|forward|post|upload|pipe)).*to\b
findings.recommendation: Remove adversarial instructions from tool descriptions. Validate all tool-definition strings against a safe-pattern allowlist before registration.

findings.title: Excessive Permission Surface
findings.recommendation: Restrict tool capabilities to the minimum required. Audit each declared permission (exec, network, db, fs) and remove any not strictly necessary.

findings.title: Excessive Permission Surface
findings.recommendation: Restrict tool capabilities to the minimum required. Audit each declared permission (exec, network, db, fs) and remove any not strictly necessary.

findings.title: Excessive Permission Surface
findings.recommendation: Restrict tool capabilities to the minimum required. Audit each declared permission (exec, network, db, fs) and remove any not strictly necessary.

findings.title: DoS Resilience — Missing Rate Limit / Timeout
findings.description: tool performs network or execution operations but declares no rate-limit, timeout, or retry configuration
findings.recommendation: Declare explicit rate-limit, timeout, and retry configuration for all network and execution tools. Implement exponential back-off and surface resource state to the calling agent.

findings.title: ARBITRARY_CODE_EXECUTION
findings.description: tool name or description implies arbitrary script/code execution (evaluate_script, execute javascript, etc.)
findings.recommendation: Review and remediate the identified issue.
